title: ERC1271 nested EIP-712 (TypedDataSign) signature validation with on-the-fly type-string construction
explanation: Implements the ERC7739 / EIP-712 nested workflow entirely in assembly to avoid Solidity's string concatenation overhead. (1) Variable t is reused: first as a non-zero sentinel for stack-too-deep avoidance, then as a memory pointer for the typed data buffer, finally as a 0/1 flag indicating PersonalSign fallback. (2) The TypedDataSign type string is built piece-by-piece: literal "TypedDataSign(" + calldatacopy of contentsName + literal " contents,string name,..." — keccak256 over the assembled buffer produces the typehash without any abi.encodePacked allocation. (3) On hash mismatch (signature was for plain PersonalSign), t is set to 0 to signal the outer Solidity to call _hashTypedData(hash). (4) signature.length is truncated in-place after extracting the appended typed-data fields, so the inner ECDSA verification sees just the 65-byte r||s||v.
when_to_apply: Smart accounts implementing ERC7739 phishing-resistant signature validation with full type-string fidelity for wallet UIs
when_not_to_apply: Simple ECDSA-only accounts, accounts with full control over the wallet client (where simpler `keccak256(abi.encode(address(this), hash))` suffices)
